nodes.name: Claims Portal
nodes.description: Customer and adjuster-facing web portal for claims submission, tracking, and management. Supports both desktop and mobile access.

nodes.name: Claims Web UI
nodes.description: React-based single-page application for claims management. Supports both customer self-service and adjuster workflows.

nodes.name: Claims Mobile BFF
nodes.description: Backend-for-Frontend service optimized for mobile clients. Aggregates data from multiple backend services.

nodes.name: Claims Adjuster
nodes.description: Insurance professional responsible for evaluating and processing claims.

nodes.name: Policyholder
nodes.description: Customer who holds an insurance policy and may file claims.

nodes.name: First Notice of Loss
nodes.description: The ability to receive and register initial loss notifications across all channels (web, mobile, phone, email).

nodes.name: Member
nodes.description: USAA member (policyholder/claimant). Interacts with Claims Portal, Policy Admin, and Billing System via web and mobile applications.

nodes.name: External Vendor
nodes.description: Third-party service providers: body shops, medical providers, contractors. Interact via Claims Portal API for assignment acceptance and status updates.

nodes.name: Adjudication API
nodes.description: REST API service handling claim adjudication decisions. Orchestrates rules engine evaluation, fraud checks, and settlement calculations.

nodes.name: Document Storage Service
nodes.description: Manages claim-related documents including photos, PDFs, police reports, and medical records. Integrates with OCR for auto-extraction.

nodes.name: Claims Processing System
nodes.description: Core claims processing system handling end-to-end claim lifecycle from FNOL through settlement. Processes approximately 45,000 claims per month across all lines of business.
nodes.nfr_encryption: AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit

nodes.name: Reduce Claims Cycle Time
nodes.description: Reduce average claims cycle time from 14 days to 7 days by Q4 2026 through automation and process optimization.

nodes.name: FNOL Process
nodes.description: End-to-end First Notice of Loss process from initial notification through claim creation and assignment.

nodes.name: Catastrophe Claims Process
nodes.description: Specialized high-volume claims process activated during declared catastrophe events (hurricanes, hail, wildfire). Includes: CAT declaration trigger → surge staffing → field adjuster deployment → expedited FNOL intake → batch adjudication → accelerated settlement. Handles 10x normal volume with modified SLAs.

nodes.name: Improve Member Experience
nodes.description: Deliver seamless digital experiences across quoting, servicing, and claims with <3 second page loads and >4.5 app store rating.

nodes.name: Policy Admin System
nodes.description: Core policy administration system managing policy lifecycle from quoting through renewal. Handles all lines of business.

nodes.name: Billing System
nodes.description: Core billing system managing premium billing, payment processing, and accounts receivable for all lines of business.

nodes.name: Rules Engine
nodes.description: Business rules engine for claims adjudication. Contains 2,400+ rules across auto, property, and liability lines.

nodes.name: Claims Database
nodes.description: Primary claims data store. Aurora PostgreSQL with read replicas for reporting queries.

nodes.name: Event Router
nodes.description: Kafka-based event routing for claims domain events. Handles FNOL, status change, payment, and notification events.

nodes.name: REST API First
nodes.description: All new services must expose REST APIs following the enterprise API design guidelines. SOAP interfaces are prohibited for new development.

nodes.name: Adjudication Decision
nodes.description: Record of the adjudication decision including coverage determination, payment amount, and rationale.

nodes.name: Claim Record
nodes.description: Master claim record containing claim metadata, status, amounts, parties, and lifecycle events.

nodes.name: Policy Reference
nodes.description: Reference to the insurance policy associated with a claim. Includes coverage terms, limits, and deductibles.

nodes.name: ADR-0042: Real-time Adjudication
nodes.description: Decision to implement real-time adjudication for auto claims under $5,000 using a dedicated fast-path rules engine, bypassing the batch queue.

nodes.name: ADR: API Gateway Pattern
nodes.description: ADR: Implement API gateway (Kong) for external-facing APIs. Rationale: centralized auth, rate limiting, observability. Status: accepted.

nodes.name: Claims Notification Service
nodes.description: Handles email, SMS, and push notifications for claims status updates, document requests, and settlement communications.

nodes.name: Claims Reporting Service
nodes.description: Generates operational and regulatory reports. Includes daily loss runs, monthly reserve reports, and ad-hoc analytics.

nodes.name: AWS Deployment Standard
nodes.description: All new workloads must be deployed to AWS using approved patterns: ECS Fargate for services, Lambda for event handlers, RDS/Aurora for databases.

nodes.name: Payment Gateway
nodes.description: Payment processing gateway supporting credit card, ACH, and wire transfers. PCI DSS Level 1 compliant.

nodes.name: Claim Adjudication
nodes.description: The ability to evaluate a claim against policy terms, coverage rules, and fraud indicators to produce an adjudication decision.

nodes.name: Claim Settlement
nodes.description: The ability to calculate, authorize, and execute claim payments including partial payments, reserves, and recoveries.

nodes.name: Monitoring Platform
nodes.description: Centralized monitoring and observability platform. Collects metrics, logs, and traces from all systems.

nodes.name: Legacy Claims API
nodes.description: Legacy SOAP-based claims API. Scheduled for decommission by Q3 2026. Still serves 15% of integration traffic from partner systems.

nodes.name: Subrogation Recovery Process
nodes.description: End-to-end subrogation workflow: identify recovery opportunity → send demand letter → negotiate with third-party insurer → collect payment → close recovery. Average cycle time: 45 days. Recovery rate: 62% of identified opportunities.

nodes.name: Fraud Investigation Process
nodes.description: SIU investigation workflow triggered by fraud scoring above threshold (>0.7). Steps: case assignment → evidence collection → interview scheduling → determination → referral/closure. SLA: initial review within 48 hours, determination within 30 days.

nodes.name: Log Analytics Platform
nodes.description: Centralized log aggregation and analysis platform. ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) providing full-text search, log correlation, and anomaly detection across all systems.

nodes.name: Catastrophe Management
nodes.description: The ability to declare catastrophe events, activate surge protocols, deploy field resources, and manage high-volume claims processing with modified SLAs and approval authorities.

nodes.name: Observability
nodes.description: Ability to monitor, trace, and debug distributed systems through metrics, logs, and traces with real-time alerting.

nodes.name: Policy API
nodes.description: REST API for policy CRUD operations, endorsements, and renewals.

nodes.name: Policy Database
nodes.description: Primary policy data store with full history and audit trail.

nodes.name: Policy Event Bus
nodes.description: Kafka-based event bus for policy domain events (issued, renewed, cancelled, endorsed).

nodes.name: Policy Rules Engine
nodes.description: Business rules engine for underwriting decisions. Evaluates risk acceptability, coverage eligibility, and pricing exceptions using Drools-based rule sets.

nodes.name: Policy Workflow Engine
nodes.description: Orchestrates policy lifecycle workflows including new business, renewal, endorsement, and cancellation. Camunda BPM-based with custom task handlers.

nodes.name: Policy Cache
nodes.description: Redis cache layer for frequently accessed policy data, rate tables, and territory factors. Reduces database load by 70%.

nodes.name: Policy Issuance
nodes.description: The ability to issue new insurance policies across all lines of business.

nodes.name: Policy Renewal
nodes.description: The ability to process policy renewals including re-rating and document generation.

nodes.name: Underwriting
nodes.description: Ability to assess risk, determine insurability, and price policies based on risk factors, loss history, and underwriting guidelines.

nodes.name: Policy Lifecycle Management
nodes.description: Ability to manage the full policy lifecycle from quote through renewal, including endorsements, cancellations, and reinstatements.

nodes.name: New Business Underwriting Process
nodes.description: End-to-end new business workflow: application intake → risk assessment → underwriting decision → quote generation → binding → policy issuance. SLA: 48 hours for standard auto, 5 days for specialty.

nodes.name: Policy Renewal Process
nodes.description: Automated renewal process: renewal eligibility check → re-rating → renewal offer generation → member notification → acceptance/decline → policy reissue. 90-day advance processing window.

nodes.name: Endorsement Processing
nodes.description: Mid-term policy change workflow: change request → impact assessment → re-rating → approval routing → endorsement issuance → billing adjustment.

nodes.name: Underwriter
nodes.description: P&C underwriter. Uses Policy Admin System for risk assessment, exception handling, and manual review of complex applications.

nodes.name: Billing API
nodes.description: REST API for billing operations.

nodes.name: Billing Database
nodes.description: Billing data store with financial transaction history.

nodes.name: Payment Ledger
nodes.description: Double-entry accounting ledger for premium receivables, payments, refunds, and write-offs. PostgreSQL with strict ACID compliance.

nodes.name: Billing Notification Service
nodes.description: Sends billing reminders, payment confirmations, and past-due notices via email, SMS, and push notifications.

nodes.name: Billing Scheduler
nodes.description: Scheduled job runner for installment generation, auto-pay processing, and grace period enforcement. Runs nightly and on-demand.

nodes.name: Data Classification Standard
nodes.description: Data classification standard: PII handling (encryption at rest AES-256, in transit TLS 1.3), retention policies, access controls per NIST 800-53.

nodes.name: Premium Collection
nodes.description: Ability to collect premiums through multiple payment methods, manage installment plans, and handle payment failures gracefully.

nodes.name: Financial Reconciliation
nodes.description: Ability to reconcile premium receivables with actual payments, identify discrepancies, and manage refunds/write-offs.

nodes.name: Premium Billing Process
nodes.description: Monthly billing cycle: installment generation → payment collection → reconciliation → grace period management → cancellation for non-payment. Processes ~200K installments monthly.

edges.description: Vendors interact via Claims Portal API
CAT claims process realizes FNOL capability at surge scale
API gateway pattern applies to adjudication API
Retrieves policy details for claim verification
Subrogation process automated by Claims Processing System
Fraud investigation automated by Claims Processing System
CAT process automated by Claims Processing System
CAT management supports claims cycle reduction
CAT process uses Event Router for high-volume intake
CAT events trigger elevated fraud screening
CAT claims process realizes CAT management capability
Policy lifecycle realized by Policy Admin
New business automated by Policy Admin
Billing data governed by classification standard
Premium collection realized by Billing System
Premium billing automated by Billing System
